# live-user usage guide for AI agents

`live-user` is a real-time online-visitor counter that runs as a single Hono
Cloudflare Worker fronting **one Durable Object per `siteId`** (WebSocket
Hibernation API + embedded SQLite). The "online" number is NEVER stored — it is
`ctx.getWebSockets().length` on the site's DO, so it is always exact and
self-healing. Only an opt-in **total-visits** counter is persisted, in SQLite
inside that same DO. Integration is one `<script src=".../liveuser.js">` tag.

Primary docs:

- README: ./README.md
- Design spec (authoritative): ./DESIGN.md
- Runtime config: ./wrangler.jsonc
- Types: ./src/types/index.ts

Part of the `@cdlab/projects-monorepo`; run all commands from the repo root with
`pnpm --filter @cdlab/live-user <script>`.

## What it is

- **One Worker, two tiers.** Stateless Hono front (`src/index.ts`) mounting
  `homeRoutes` (`GET /`), `sdkRoutes` (`GET /liveuser.js`), `wsRoutes`
  (`GET /ws`) — all at `/`. Stateful `SiteManager` Durable Object
  (`src/site-manager.ts`) owns the sockets + SQLite.
- **Deterministic sharding.** `wsRoutes` resolves the DO with
  `SITE_MANAGER.idFromName(siteId)` → one strongly-consistent, single-threaded DO
  instance per site. This is why the SQLite increment needs no locking.
- **DO re-export.** `src/index.ts` does `export { SiteManager }` — Wrangler needs
  the class exported from the main module to bind it. Do not remove it.
- **No secrets, no env vars, no KV/R2/D1.** The only binding is the DO
  `SITE_MANAGER`.

## Connection lifecycle

1. Browser loads `/liveuser.js`; `sdkRoutes` (`src/routes/sdk.ts`) parses query →
   `SDKConfig` (`parseConfig`) and returns the `SDK_SCRIPT` IIFE with config
   JSON-injected (`application/javascript`, `no-cache`, `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`).
2. The IIFE makes a UUID `clientId`, finds `displayElementId`, opens
   `wss://host/ws?siteId=…&clientId=…[&enableTotalCount=true]`, sends
   `{type:'join'}`, starts a 30s heartbeat.
3. `wsRoutes` `GET /ws` (`src/routes/ws.ts`): `idFromName(siteId)` → stub, forward
   raw upgrade `stub.fetch(url, c.req.raw)`. It rewrites only `clientId` +
   `enableTotalCount` into the URL, NOT `siteId` (DO re-reads it from the
   still-present original query).
4. `SiteManager.fetch`: non-`/ws` → 404; else `WebSocketPair` +
   `ctx.acceptWebSocket(server)` + `serializeAttachment(state)`; if
   `enableTotalCount` → `incrementTotalCount`; `broadcast(siteId)`; return 101.
5. `webSocketMessage`: `heartbeat` → reply live count (+totalCount if this socket
   opted in); `join` → broadcast. State via `ws.deserializeAttachment()`.
6. `webSocketClose` / `webSocketError` → re-broadcast.

## Key files

- `src/index.ts` — Hono app, `logger`+`requestId` middleware, route mounts, 404
  JSON `{statusCode,message}`, DO re-export, `export default {fetch: app.fetch}`.
- `src/site-manager.ts` — `SiteManager` DO; the entire stateful core.
- `src/routes/sdk.ts` — `/liveuser.js`; `parseConfig` + the `SDK_SCRIPT` IIFE.
- `src/routes/ws.ts` — `/ws`; DO resolution + upgrade forwarding.
- `src/routes/home.tsx` — `GET /` renders `<HomePage>` via Hono JSX.
- `src/pages/{Layout,HomePage}.tsx` — JSX HTML shell + self-demo page (embeds its
  own counter on `siteId=official-website`).
- `src/types/index.ts` — `AppEnv`, `ConnectionState`, `SDKConfig`.

## Data model (SQLite inside the DO)

Created idempotently in the DO constructor under `ctx.blockConcurrencyWhile`:

```
visit_counter(site_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0)
```

- Read: `getTotalCount` — `SELECT count … WHERE site_id = ?`, missing → 0.
- Increment: `incrementTotalCount` — `INSERT … VALUES(?,1) ON CONFLICT(site_id)
  DO UPDATE SET count = count + 1` (atomic upsert).
- Online count is NOT stored — derived from `ctx.getWebSockets().length`.

## Config & bindings

- SDK/runtime config is read from the `/liveuser.js` query string (`parseConfig`,
  `src/routes/sdk.ts`): `serverUrl` (`ws(s)://host/`), `siteId` (`default-site`),
  `displayElementId` (`liveuser`), `totalCountElementId` (`liveuser_totalvisits`),
  `reconnectDelay` (3000), `debug` (false), `enableTotalCount` (false).
- Hard-coded in the SDK: 30s heartbeat, K/M format thresholds.
- Binding: `SITE_MANAGER` DO → class `SiteManager` (`wrangler.jsonc`; `AppEnv`).
  Migration tag `v1` = `new_sqlite_classes: ["SiteManager"]`. `account_id` is
  EMPTY — set it before deploy.

## Gotchas for agents

- **Never `ws.close()` in `webSocketClose`** — the Hibernation API fires it AFTER
  the socket is already closed; calling close there throws. Only `webSocketError`
  closes (with `ws.close(1011, …)`), then broadcasts. See DESIGN §4.2.
- **Store state on the socket, not the DO instance.** Use
  `serializeAttachment`/`deserializeAttachment` for `ConnectionState`; the DO can
  hibernate/evict between events. Handlers must be top-level DO methods, not
  closures.
- **`broadcast` reads EACH recipient's own `enableTotalCount`** from its
  attachment, not the triggering connection's flag — opted-out clients must never
  receive `totalCount`. Same rule in the heartbeat reply. See DESIGN §5.
- **totalCount only increments on `enableTotalCount=true` connections** — it is
  connection-count, not unique visitors. Sites embedding without the flag never
  accrue totals.
- **Don't add `siteId` rewriting in `wsRoutes`** — the DO re-reads `siteId` from
  the original forwarded query; only `clientId`/`enableTotalCount` are rewritten.
- **Doc discrepancy:** `Layout.tsx` SEO/JSON-LD says "SSE" but the transport is
  WebSockets. WebSockets is source of truth.
- No auth, no rate limiting, no secrets — `/liveuser.js` and `/ws` are open to any
  origin (`Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`).
- No test suite exists.

## Common commands

```bash
pnpm --filter @cdlab/live-user dev         # http://live-user.localhost:3355 (nsl)
pnpm --filter @cdlab/live-user cf-typegen  # regenerate CloudflareBindings types
pnpm --filter @cdlab/live-user deploy      # wrangler deploy --minify
```
